title: MFFC — the "private cone" of a node

steps.title: Goal: which ANDs are private to f?
steps.narrative:
An MFFC (Maximum Fanout-Free Cone) of a node n is the set of internal AIG ANDs whose only consumer (directly or transitively) is n. They exist solely to feed n; remove n and they become unreachable.

This circuit has two outputs, f and g. Crucially, n1 = a&b is shared between them — it feeds both f and g. So n1 is not private to f, even though f's cone includes it. The other internal ANDs (n2, n3, n4) only feed f, so they are.

entries.n1: a · b (used by f and g)

steps.title: Boundary boost on cut leaves {a, b, c, d}
steps.narrative: MFFC w.r.t. the PI-support cut: +1 each PI's fanout count so deref recursion stops at the cut.

steps.title: Deref f → count = 1, mark f
steps.narrative: deref(f): count = 1, label f with the current TravId. Then walk into f's two fanins (n1 and n4).

steps.title: Deref n1 (f's fanin0): 2 → 1, NOT zero — n1 survives
steps.narrative: f.fanin0 = n1. Decrement n1's fanout count: 2 → 1. NOT zero — n1 still has another consumer (g). The recursion does not enter n1, and n1 is not added to the MFFC. This is the moment that distinguishes MFFC from "everything in the cone".

entries.Verdict: n1 stays alive — has external fanout to g

panes.body: Visually, n1 is now outlined green to mark it as "considered, not absorbed" — its outgoing edge to g (not shown highlighted) keeps its count above zero.

steps.title: Deref n4 (f's fanin1): 1 → 0 → recurse
steps.narrative: f.fanin1 = n4. Decrement: 1 → 0 → recurse into n4. n4's only consumer was f, so it joins the MFFC.

steps.title: Inside deref(n4): n2 → 0, recurse; n2's fanins are leaves
steps.narrative: n4.fanin0 = n2 → 1 → 0 → recurse. n2's fanins are a and c, both cut leaves. They decrement (3→2 each) but stay positive — no recursion past the cut.

steps.title: Inside deref(n4): n3 → 0, recurse; cut leaves decrement
steps.narrative: n4.fanin1 = n3 → 1 → 0 → recurse. n3's fanins are b, c, both cut leaves.

steps.title: Result: MFFC = {f, n4, n2, n3}, nNodesSaved = 4
steps.narrative:
Recursion done. The MFFC is the four red nodes — exactly the ANDs that exist solely to compute f. n1 is green (survived: shared with g) and the entire g-subnetwork is untouched.

If a replacement for f were committed, those four red nodes would be deleted; n1 and g would stay. That's why nNodesSaved = 4 (not the entire AIG size).

entries.Survived: n1 (green) — fanout to g protected it
entries.Untouched: g, d, PO_g — outside f's cone

panes.title: Re-reference pass
panes.body: Abc_NodeMffcLabelAig follows up with a re-ref pass that walks the same red path in reverse, restoring every fanout count. Then the boundary boost on a, b, c, d is stripped. The AIG is byte-identical to its original state — only the red nodes carry the current TravId, which downstream code (Dec_GraphToNetworkCount, §decgraph) uses to test "is this node about to die?" in O(1).
